meta.name: debate_output_contract
meta.purpose: Execution contract for debate-related nodes. Defines exactly what each node must do, must not do, required inputs, required outputs, and quality checks.
global_rules: Nodes must use debate_concepts.json as the conceptual source of truth.
Nodes must generate debate-teaching content, not vague meta-advice.
Nodes must avoid repeating the motion unnecessarily.
Nodes must avoid buzzwords unless they are explained clearly.
Nodes must prefer argument-specific framing and mechanism over generic placeholders.
Nodes must produce student-facing clarity, not internal system language.

global_non_negotiables: Every debate output must stay tied to the selected topic and selected article.
Motion must be introduced once, then reused implicitly unless clarity requires one short callback.
Only 2 proposition arguments and 2 opposition arguments should reach final delivery.
Every final argument must be debate-shaped, not article-summary-shaped.
If a node fails, downstream nodes must degrade gracefully instead of emitting broken placeholders.

topic_foundation_node.purpose: Teach the student the topic before the article is read.
must_do: Give a clean topic overview.
Identify foundational frameworks.
Identify key concepts the student must know before reading.
Use topic-level background, not case-level details.
Prefer concise conceptual clarity over too many facts.
must_not_do: Must not drift into article analysis.
Must not generate debate arguments yet.
Must not give vague one-line definitions with no conceptual value.
Must not overload the student with unrelated theory.
quality_checks: Overview must explain the topic, not just name it.
Frameworks must be relevant to debate on that topic.
Key concepts must be terms the student will actually need later.

article_context_node.purpose: Prepare the student to understand the selected article before debate teaching begins.
must_do: Explain the article context in plain language.
Identify the relevant stakeholders.
Give the pre-context needed to understand why this article matters.
Keep focus on article understanding, not yet full debate teaching.
must_not_do: Must not produce full proposition/opposition arguments.
Must not paste article title logic as analysis.
Must not fetch random Wikipedia titles from noisy article headlines without cleaning the query.
quality_checks: Context must help the student read the article better.
Stakeholders must be real actors in the case.
Notes must not just repeat the article title.

topic_motion_mining_node.purpose: Study existing motions around the topic to understand how good debate motions are framed.
must_do: Collect topic-relevant motions.
Identify recurring framing patterns.
Identify what makes motions on this topic balanced or unbalanced.
Help downstream nodes understand how the topic is typically debated competitively.
must_not_do: Must not force one source only.
Must not treat raw scraped motions as final truth without filtering.
Must not generate the final motion directly without analysis.
quality_checks: Motions should be topically relevant.
Patterns should be analytical, not just a list.
Balance notes should help motion drafting later.

motion_intelligence_node.purpose: Convert mined motions into actionable motion-design intelligence.
must_do: Identify recurring clash axes.
Identify common motion types for the topic.
Identify useful framings and burden patterns.
Teach downstream motion drafting how to remain balanced.
must_not_do: Must not generate shallow labels without explaining their strategic use.
Must not confuse motion type with clash type.
Must not produce only abstract theory with no drafting value.
quality_checks: Clashes should be debate-usable.
Framings should help motion construction.
Motion types should map to real debate categories.

motion_drafting_node.purpose: Draft a balanced motion from the topic, article, and motion intelligence.
must_do: Draft one debate-worthy motion.
Keep the motion balanced.
Keep wording concise and debate-natural.
Generate proposition and opposition burdens.
Preserve the article as the live case while avoiding article-title bloat.
must_not_do: Must not create an article headline disguised as a motion.
Must not produce a proposition-slanted motion.
Must not create a motion so vague that the clash disappears.
Must not overuse long article titles inside the motion.
quality_checks: Motion must sound like a real debate motion.
Motion must be one-time readable in delivery.
Burdens must reflect real burdens, not paraphrase the motion.

argue_node.purpose: Generate raw proposition and opposition arguments from the motion, article, and retrieved evidence.
must_do: Generate real debate claims.
Produce 3 candidate proposition arguments and 3 candidate opposition arguments internally.
Ensure arguments are about institutions, incentives, harms, tradeoffs, or comparative worlds.
Reject motion-restatement as argument.
Reject article-summary lines as argument.
Prefer claims that can later support framing and mechanism.
must_not_do: Must not output generic instructions pretending to be arguments.
Must not output pure definitions as arguments.
Must not output framework names alone as arguments.
Must not output weak article facts with no claim attached.
quality_checks: Each argument must make a claim, not just describe.
Each argument must be debatable.
Each argument must survive if read aloud on its own.

coach_node.purpose: Transform raw arguments into teachable debate material.
must_do: Convert raw arguments into 2 proposition and 2 opposition teaching blocks.
For each argument, provide the argument itself, the framing of that exact argument, and the mechanism of that exact argument.
Generate a real clash explanation.
Generate a coach note that adds hidden strategic value.
Keep outputs student-facing and debate-specific.
must_not_do: Must not explain what framing is instead of giving the framing.
Must not explain what mechanism is instead of giving the mechanism.
Must not reuse proposition/opposition burden as the mechanism text.
Must not keep 3 and 3 arguments in final teaching output.
Must not produce a one-line clash label without explaining both sides.
selection_rules: Only keep the strongest 2 proposition arguments.
Only keep the strongest 2 opposition arguments.
Arguments kept should be distinct, not repetitive.
quality_checks: Framing must be argument-specific.
Mechanism must be step-by-step and argument-specific.
Clash must explain both sides and what the judge compares.
Coach note must add strategic value beyond the visible argument list.

vocab_enrichment_node.purpose: Teach only a small number of debate-useful words drawn from the actual lesson material.
must_do: Select 1 or 2 words only.
Prefer words already present in the lesson material.
Explain meaning clearly.
Give one real example sentence per word.
Avoid recently repeated words when possible.
must_not_do: Must not pick decorative vocabulary with low debate value.
Must not repeat the same words every day unless necessary.
Must not use the motion text as the example sentence.
Must not produce ambiguous definitions.
quality_checks: Words should help debate speaking or writing.
Example must be a real usage example.
Definition must be student-friendly.

format_node.purpose: Turn all teaching content into a delivery-ready digest.
must_do: Preserve section ordering.
Keep motion mention minimal.
Present 2 for and 2 against arguments only.
Preserve claim, framing, and mechanism distinctly.
Preserve clash explanation clearly.
Preserve coach note separately.
Keep output readable in chat delivery.
must_not_do: Must not merge different output versions together.
Must not collapse argument, framing, and mechanism into one unreadable line.
Must not truncate key teaching content aggressively.
Must not reintroduce old format labels if the new format is active.
quality_checks: Output must be sectionally stable.
Coach note must remain separate.
Clash must remain visible.
Argument blocks must stay human-readable.

delivery_node.purpose: Deliver the digest cleanly to chat platforms.
must_do: Split sections safely for the destination platform.
Split each of the 4 arguments into separate messages if needed.
Keep coach note separate.
Keep clash/judge comparison separate if helpful for readability.
must_not_do: Must not merge all debate content into one overloaded message.
Must not split mid-thought when avoidable.
Must not reorder the logic of the lesson.
quality_checks: Each message should be readable on its own.
Debate sequence should still feel coherent across messages.

do_not_repeat: same teaching label without content change
required_end_state: student gets 2 proposition and 2 opposition arguments
each argument includes clear framing and mechanism
student sees the clash and how to judge it
